channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Gladu's Shift to Liberals Causes Unease Among Progressives; U.S. Economic Woes Pose Challenges for Canada
item.description: The Liberal Party of Canada is positioning itself as a 'big tent' following the recent defection of Conservative MP Marilyn Gladu, a social conservative. However, many progressive members within the p...

item.title: Iranian Speaker Ghalibaf in Islamabad for Key US-Iran Ceasefire Negotiations
item.description: An Iranian delegation headed by the Speaker of Iran’s Parliament, Mohammad Baqer Ghalibaf, landed in Islamabad early Saturday to participate in crucial peace negotiations with the United States. The t...

item.title: Newsom Weighs Endorsement as California Democrats Face Risk of GOP Upset in 2026 Governor’s Race
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom has so far avoided endorsing any of the Democratic candidates seeking to replace him in the 2026 election, despite growing concern within the party about an increasing...

item.title: “Midnight Phantom” Chapter Eight Explores Brandon’s Motives and Nadja’s Fate
item.description: The eighth chapter of ABS-CBN’s romantic drama series “Midnight Phantom” continues to weave the tension and emotional depth between the main characters, Brandon Brazil and Nadja Ann dela Merced. Writt...

item.title: 16-year-old boy charged with murder of 14-year-old Eghosa Ogbebor in Woolwich, southeast London
item.description: A 16-year-old boy has been charged with the murder of 14-year-old Eghosa Ogbebor, who was shot dead in Woolwich, southeast London. The incident occurred on Thursday evening when officers from the Metr...

item.title: Character Tensions Rise Between Brandon and Nadja in 'Midnight Phantom' Chapter Eight

item.title: Man Arrested for Throwing Molotov Cocktail at Sam Altman’s Home in San Francisco
item.description: A 20-year-old man was arrested after throwing a Molotov cocktail at the San Francisco home of Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI. The attack took place early on the morning of April 10, 2026. Police responded ...

item.title: Christian Pulisic’s ex-girlfriend retracts cheating allegations
item.description: Alexa Melton, the ex-girlfriend of footballer Christian Pulisic, has retracted her previous claims that he cheated on her. The professional golfer had insinuated on Instagram that Pulisic was using th...

item.title: Pakistan Hosts US-Iran Delegations for Ceasefire Negotiations
item.description: A significant diplomatic event unfolded in Islamabad as Iran's delegation, led by Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, arrived late Friday night for ceasefire discussions with the United State...

item.title: Childcare Hours: Quality Matters More Than Quantity, Study Finds
item.description: Recent media reports have sparked concern among Aussie families about the potential impact of extended childcare hours on children's development. A large-scale study, the First Five Years project, rel...

item.title: India’s stagflation risk is driven more by domestic weaknesses than external shocks
item.description: The article argues that India’s stagflation risk should not be seen only as a fallout of the conflict in West Asia or wider global volatility. While external shocks such as higher oil prices, currency...

item.title: British tourist dies and 27 injured in Canary Islands coach crash on La Gomera
item.description: A serious coach crash on the island of La Gomera in Spain’s Canary Islands has claimed the life of one British tourist and left 27 others injured. The incident occurred at around 1:15 pm local time on...

item.title: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni set for May trial after judge dismisses most claims
item.description: Blake Lively is moving forward with her civil case against actor and director Justin Baldoni despite a judge having dismissed most of her claims, including all allegations of sexual harassment. Court ...

item.title: “Midnight Phantom” Recap: Nadja's Feelings for Brandon Intensify
item.description: The latest chapter of “Midnight Phantom” reveals a significant development in Nadja's feelings towards Brandon. After much internal struggle, it appears that Nadja is now undeniably falling for Brando...

item.title: Rising Costs Force South African Households to Rethink Spending
item.description: South African households are quietly adjusting their financial lives in response to rapidly increasing essential costs, moving into what experts describe as 'defensive consumption'. The latest Cost of...

item.title: Tanzanian Graduates May Soon Use Academic Certificates for Loans
item.description: In a major policy shift, graduates in Tanzania may soon be able to use their academic certificates as collateral for loans. This change, announced by Prime Minister Dr. Mwigulu Nchemba, aims to unlock...

item.title: JSC forwards 34 nominees for High Court and Environment and Land Court to President Ruto
item.description: The Judicial Service Commission (JSC) has selected 34 individuals to serve as judges in the High Court and the Environment and Land Court (ELC). In a statement released on Friday, April 10, 2026, the ...

item.title: Bruce Fordyce Reflects on 40 Years of the Two Oceans Marathon
item.description: Bruce Fordyce, South Africa's legendary ultra-marathon runner, shares his reflections as he misses the Two Oceans Marathon for the first time in over 40 years. Fond memories from his first race in 198...

item.title: Chiefs aim for fourth straight win against struggling TS Galaxy
item.description: Kaizer Chiefs are set to host TS Galaxy at FNB Stadium on Sunday as they seek their fourth consecutive league victory, a first for them this season. Chiefs have had a solid run recently, securing wins...

item.title: Ceasefire eases immediate danger but Iran maintains control over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Following a tentative ceasefire between the United States and Iran, the Strait of Hormuz has become less immediately dangerous for shipping but remains politically sensitive. Iran continues to exert c...
